description: The first Asian man to reach a Grand Slam final is calling time. Kei Nishikori, the 2014 US Open finalist, Rio 2016 bronze medallist and former world No. 4, announced on Thursday that he will retire at the end of 2026. "I still wish I could continue my playing career," the 36-year-old said. "Even so, I can proudly say that I gave it my all."
